entry.title: Build, Deploy, and Extend AI Agents with Joule Studio
entry.content:
Build, Deploy, and Extend AI Agents with Joule Studio

Today, we’re excited to share more details about Joule Studio, a new capability in SAP Build designed specifically for creating custom AI agents and Joule skills powered by Agentic AI. With Joule Studio, you can easily design, deploy, and manage intelligent agents uniquely tailored to your organization’s processes and data. It’s a significant step forward in enabling you to apply AI to tackle real-world challenges, streamline operations, and deliver tangible business impact.

“Joule Studio has helped us optimize resource allocation, improve time management, and enhance collaboration by using Agentic AI for our project management workflows. We have also used Joule Studio to enhance our AGIL AI.TimeManager application for time and activity management.”  
- Thomas Neuhaus, Head of Digital Experience Services, AGILITA Switzerland / Germany 

What is Joule Studio?

Traditional automation often falls short of addressing the complexity and dynamism of modern enterprises. While agents can think, reason, and adapt like skilled team members, they typically struggle with understanding business context and data, limiting their effectiveness across diverse landscapes.

Joule Studio, a capability of SAP Build, enables you to create purpose-built Joule agents and skills. It brings together SAP’s business process expertise and data from its comprehensive set of Cloud ERP applications with Agentic AI. Leveraging SAP Business Data Cloud and SAP Knowledge Graph, these agents gain semantic access to business-relevant information across both SAP and non-SAP applications, empowering them to perform their tasks with greater context and precision. As part of SAP Build, agents can also interact with workflows, automations, and custom applications, and be embedded into your own solutions to extend and enhance business processes.

Here’s what you can build in Joule Studio with its drag-and-drop simplicity:

  • AI Agents that are capable of planning, reasoning, and executing complex business scenarios. Imagine an agent optimizing your logistics network, providing risk mitigation strategies, making procurement recommendations, and sourcing candidates for interviews—all while adapting to evolving requirements.
  • Joule Skills that are designed to handle repetitive, rule-based tasks, freeing your teams from routine activities such as data entry, IT ticket raising, and PO creation, thereby boosting productivity.

Both agents and skills are seamlessly orchestrated and triggered based on user intent and the context of conversations within Joule Copilot, delivering an intuitive end-user experience.


Image 1: Create an AI agent and Joule Skill project in SAP Build


Deeply Grounded in Business Process and Data: The SAP Advantage

One of the biggest challenges in enterprise AI is grounding agents in the context of your business. Many agents fail because they lack access to the correct data and don’t understand the nuances of your business. AI agents built with Joule Studio are deeply grounded in your business:

  • Data Grounding: Agents are anchored in your unique enterprise knowledge through SAP Business Data Cloud and SAP Knowledge Graph. This ensures they understand not just the ‘what’ but also the ‘why’ behind every task, leading to more reliable outcomes.
  • Process Integration: Easily embed existing workflows, automations, integrations, and business logic from SAP Build into your agents and skills, streamlining cross-functional workflows.
  • Role Specialization: Tailor agents for specific business areas and roles, whether it’s finance, HR, supply chain, or customer service, so they align closely to your business needs.


Image 2: Provide instructions to the agent in natural language to define its goal and role


Open and Extensible Framework

Enterprises operate across multiple platforms and technologies. Joule Studio is designed to be open and extensible, allowing your AI agents to function across heterogeneous landscapes:

  • Connect to all your systems: Integrate your agents with external systems, APIs, and AI models, extending their reach beyond the SAP ecosystem. For instance, an agent could pull supply chain risk data from a third-party provider, analyze it, and offer supplier recommendations for procurement.
  • API Management and Governance: Use SAP Integration Suite to compose, govern, and monitor APIs exposed to agents and skills, including real-time usage analytics, anomaly detection, and predictive insights.
  • Flexible Model Choice: Choose from a range of out-of-the-box large language models or bring your own, ensuring your agents have the right reasoning and planning capabilities for your unique needs.


Image 3: Select from your existing action projects in SAP Build to connect to third-party systems

“Joule Studio in SAP Build is enabling teams to enrich Joule with client-specific skills and AI agents, seamlessly integrating it with the client’s application ecosystem, partner solutions, and agent network. This makes Joule a go-to solution for maximizing value and delivering a unified, agentic user experience.” 
- Stephanie Guimbellot, Global SAP Innovation & Sustainability Lead at Accenture 

Built-in Security and Compliance

Trust, transparency, and compliance are essential for AI agents. Joule Studio is built with these principles at its core:

  • Transparency: Gain complete visibility into agent planning, decision paths, and execution flows, so you can understand not just what your agents did, but why.
  • Centralized Lifecycle Management: Manage, deploy, and maintain all your agents and skills centrally within SAP Build, streamlining operations and updates.
  • Test and Validation: Test new agents and skills before deploying them into production, ensuring quality, reliability, and alignment with your business policies.
  • Compliance by Design: Ensure compliant interactions with AI using built-in data privacy controls and enforcement mechanisms.


Image 4: Test your agents and skills before deploying to minimize runtime errors.

Get a Closer Look at Joule Studio

Harness Agentic AI for your business with Joule Studio, empowering your teams to create AI agents and skills that accelerate innovation, boost productivity, and enhance decision-making. Custom Joule Skills will be generally available in June 2025 and custom AI Agents later in the year.


Want to learn more?

  • Register for the webinar: https://url.sap/2iuj2p
  • Take a guided product tour: https://url.sap/product-tour
  • Learn how to build Joule skills with this Interactive Value Journey: https://url.sap/skilldesign

entry.title: How LeverX enhances CAD Data Search for Manufacturing with Joule Studio in SAP Build
entry.content:
LeverX creates intelligent CAD data discovery with Joule Studio in SAP Build

For more than 20 years, LeverX has been helping companies across industries implement, integrate, and customize SAP solutions to meet their unique business needs. Among our many focus areas is manufacturing, where efficient data management plays a crucial role in organizations’ day-to-day productivity.

As an early-stage adopter of Joule Studio, we had the opportunity to explore its capabilities in a project inspired by a semiconductor equipment manufacturer’s real-world problem  — and find out how Joule Studio could help overcome their data management challenges in engineering environments.

Why We Chose Joule Studio

Companies approach us with a wide range of challenges, and in every case, we aim to find innovative, effective solutions that not only solve our clients’  immediate problems but also help them reach their strategic goals.

When SAP introduced Joule Studio in SAP Build for early-stage exploration, we immediately saw an opportunity to address one of the persistent pain points in the manufacturing sector: managing and accessing technical data stored within CAD files.

At our client’s company, CAD engineers spent around 4 hours each week searching for technical data hidden in complex file structures, managing analytics, and organizing design files.

Additionally, around 30% of models (components) are created from scratch rather than utilizing existing components as a foundation, leading to extra time spent on design and potential delays in manufacturing and supply chain processes.

Without a guided design process — one that would pre-define attributes based on historical data and previous experience — overall design time increased by approximately 20%. This manual effort slowed down production, contributed to redundant design work, and affected project timelines, in general.

Although SAP systems handled structured data well, properties stored within CAD files were not easily searchable through standard SAP tools. This gap created an opportunity to test Joule Studio's ability to bridge structured and unstructured data.

Building the Solution

Having previous expertise with SAP Build and SAP BTP solutions, we were excited to be among the first to explore Joule Studio’s capabilities. Joule Studio builds on the familiar low-code and no-code tools of SAP Build, providing us with a streamlined and intuitive environment for developing custom Joule skills and AI Agents.

Custom Joule skills developed in Joule studio are natively designed to work with Joule's conversational AI interface, adapting to user intent effortlessly.

Thus, our goal was to develop a custom Joule  skill that would make it easier for engineers to search and retrieve hidden CAD data — enabling smarter, faster decisions directly within SAP Joule. 

Our solution combined several technologies:

  • DataLark acted as the data extractor and parser, pulling CAD file properties from storage into SAP BTP.
  • SAP AI Coreto manage the machine learning processes and ensure accurate parsing of CAD data
  • Joule Studio was used to create a custom Joule skill integrated into Joule.

To better understand the technical setup behind our solution, have a look at the architecture overview below:

Solution Architecture

The custom Joule skill allowed engineers to search and find CAD file properties that would otherwise remain hidden to standard SAP search tools. In our demo scenario, a Bill of Materials (BOM) Engineer used SAP Joule alongside the "Maintain Bill of Material" application in SAP S/4HANA to:

  1. Plan a BOM with related materials and parameters.
  2. Use the Joule assistant to locate necessary CAD models with the right attributes.
  3. Quickly create a complete and accurate BOM based on the search results.

 

With this setup, we projected potential savings of around 10% of time of each CAD engineer. Over time, this could contribute to faster project deliveries, fewer redundant designs, and more efficient supply chains.

Our Insights and Results

Throughout the project, we found that managing the variety and complexity of CAD file properties was a challenging but rewarding task. Since different models contained diverse property structures, developing a search mechanism that worked reliably across all cases required careful design.

Our solution demonstrated strong potential during early-stage testing. While some fine-tuning will be needed for broader production use, especially as Joule Studio evolves toward General Availability, the initial results 一 savings of up to 4 hours per week per CAD engineer 一 confirmed the value and innovation opportunities Joule Studio offers for unstructured data management in engineering environments.

Looking Ahead: Preparing for AI Agents and New Possibilities With Joule Studio

Even at this early stage, the project showed how Joule Studio could open new possibilities for managing unstructured data across both SAP and non-SAP landscapes. With its extensibility to third-party systems like CAD management tools, Joule Studio is well-equipped to deliver more comprehensive, cross-system intelligence and automation. Looking forward, Joule Studio’s upcoming AI Agent builder will unlock advanced capabilities. For instance, businesses will be able to deploy agents that collaborate intelligently across systems, automate multi-step tasks, and provide proactive recommendations—all while being grounded in a company’s unique data.

At LeverX, we are excited to continue exploring how Joule Studio and AI Agents can bring even more intelligence and automation into daily business processes — not only for CAD engineers but across many other roles that deal with complex, unstructured data.

Want to experience transformation like this at your company? Explore Joule Studio within SAP Build and see how you can drive automation and improve user productivity today!

entry.title: How Pricefx is transforming pricing software with Joule Studio in SAP Build
entry.content:
About Pricefx

At Pricefx, we specialize in pricing management and optimization software, serving industries like discrete manufacturing, process and chemical sectors, and distribution. Our customers range from mid-sized enterprises to global corporations, all seeking to streamline pricing strategies and boost profitability. Our projects typically involve integrating our pricing solutions with enterprise systems like SAP Sales Cloud to empower sales teams with actionable insights. While we hadn’t used SAP Build before, diving into Joule Studio as early-stage adopters was our first hands-on experience with SAP’s low-code tools, and we were impressed by its intuitive setup.

Why We Chose Joule Studio

AI Assistants and Copilots are reshaping how businesses operate, and at Pricefx, we’re no strangers to this trend. We’ve been developing our own Agentic AI Copilot to enhance our pricing software, focusing on delivering value through seamless integration with SAP’s ecosystem.

The use case we targeted was clear: empowering sales representatives with a daily dashboard where they could ask Joule questions like, “Which customers should I prioritize today?” or “What product recommendations fit this customer?” By connecting SAP Sales Cloud with Pricefx’s pricing engine through Joule Studio to Joule, we aimed to enable real-time recommendations, price suggestions, and negotiation guidance — helping drive better margins and more effective sales processes.

How We Used Joule Studio

Joule Studio opened up new possibilities for bringing Pricefx’s pricing knowledge with SAP’s AI landscape. A key challenge we faced was teaching Joule to properly understand pricing-related queries — so that it could recognize what the sales reps were asking and pull the right information from our systems. Joule Skills became the foundation for solving this. With custom Joule skills, we were able to define specific types of requests, map them to Pricefx’s data sources, and guide Joule to respond accurately based on the user’s intent.

Before building the Joule Skill, we first connected Pricefx’s pricing data to SAP Build Actions to make it API-consumable. This setup allowed Joule to easily access the necessary information when executing custom Joule skills, ensuring smooth and reliable data retrieval during conversations.

Out of the many Joule skills we explored during our early work with Joule Studio, one standout example was our product recommendation skill.

Pricefx Product Recommendation

 

This seamless interaction not only saves time but also ensures sales reps have data-driven suggestions at their fingertips. Developing this skill in Joule Studio was surprisingly efficient, thanks to its low-code environment, which let us focus on functionality rather than complex coding.

Looking Ahead with Joule Studio

Our experience with Joule Studio has reinforced our commitment to deepening our SAP partnership. The tool’s low-code approach aligns perfectly with our vision of self-service solutions, making it easier for users to leverage pricing insights without technical barriers. We were particularly impressed by how quickly we could build and deploy Joule skills, which speaks to the power of Joule Studio’s design-time.

In 2025 and beyond, we’re excited to take this further. With SAP’s AI Agents on the horizon in the very near future, we see huge potential to create end-to-end workflows — from insights to recommendations to actions. Our plan is to use Joule Studio to develop AI Agents that help our partners act on pricing data more effectively. This focus on actionable outcomes will continue to drive innovation in how pricing software integrates with daily operations.

We’re thrilled to be part of this journey and can’t wait to see where Joule Studio takes us next.

entry.title: How sovanta uses Joule Studio to take ticketing systems to the next level
entry.content:
Real-World Use Case with SAP Joule Studio

In an era where efficiency and intelligent automation are critical, SAP has introduced Joule – a powerful AI assistant that is redefining the way users interact with SAP systems. But what do real-world use cases look like when it comes to optimizing business processes and supporting employees across departments? As an early-stage adopter of Joule Studio, sovanta put the GenAI tool through its paces. From Extension to Integration to AI – sovanta has been consulting and supporting SAP customers in the cloud environment for 15 years and has developed into a specialized SAP BTP partner. Thanks to our experienced BTP, UX, AI, and Development experts, we have established ourselves as a reliable BTP implementation partner, focusing on innovative, easy-to-use and AI-powered business apps that semelssly integrate with various systems.

Bringing together our SAP and AI experience, we successfully implemented the first use case with Joule Studio. The result? An exciting use case in the Center domain. In this blog post, we’ll explore the background of that use case and how our clients can benefit from Joule going forward.

Joule Studio in SAP Build: What It Can Do

First things first: We took a deep dive into Joule Studio within SAP Build, and we’re excited by what we found. The combination of generative AI and deep integration into SAP systems already makes Joule a powerful tool for our clients. But the real magic happens with the addition of Custom Joule Skills via Joule Studio. These enable truly impactful use cases that can make a tangible difference for businesses.

A particularly interesting aspect – and challenge – is Joule’s chat history. Joule automatically logs conversations to autonomously select the appropriate skill for follow-up questions. However, it heavily depends on the descriptions of each skill to correctly trigger them. As Joule Studio pioneer, we observed that Joule occasionally struggles to trigger the most relevant skill for certain follow-ups. By fine-tuning our skill descriptions, we were able to significantly improve precision and context handling. We are excited to see that SAP is actively working to enhance Skill Matching capabilities, and partner feedback like ours is directly shaping the ongoing evolution of Joule Studio’s intelligence.

The Use Case: Ticketing System Integration

To fully explore Joule Studio’s potential, we developed a realistic use case focused on service centers. This scenario showcases how Joule can retrieve service case information by integrating with an external ticketing system and leveraging Retrieval Augmented Generation (RAG) for contextual insights. This allows users to instantly access relevant ticket data and receive solution recommendations based on similar past cases – even without relying on Joule’s Document Grounding capabilities, which were not yet available during our early adopter experience.

The Business Challenge

When a customer reports an issue with a product, service teams are confronted with a lot of challenges:

  • Understanding the specifics of the incoming service case, extracting key information from it
  • Quickly finding internal documents that may be relevant to the customer issue
  • Checking if a similar issue was previously reported or resolved

All of these actions require the service team to navigate between various different systems, having to copy and paste information and search terms across these.

The Solution and its Key Benefits

This is where the power of Joule Studio and its ability to connect Joule with various systems comes in handy. Making full use of custom skills, we connected:

  • A ticketing system (e.g., JIRA) via API to retrieve and update service tickets.
  • The sovanta GenAI DocumentChat via API to perform RAG using historical case data.
    Good to know: While native RAG orchestration via Document Grounding is still evolving, Joule Studio already makes it easy to integrate external RAG services through APIs as part of a custom Joule Skill. In our case, we demonstrated how external RAG can be connected— using the sovanta GenAI DocumentChat to bring additional context and insights into the conversation.

We allow Joule to interact with these systems via three separate Joule Skills:

  • Skill 1: Search Tickets: Allows Joule to search for specific Tickets in the ticketing System.
  • Skill 2: Find Past Solutions: Use RAG via a context in the Document Chat that contains past solutions.
  • Skill 3: Update Ticket: Allows Joule to update a specific ticket in the ticketing System.

The flow below shows how these skills work together in a real conversation.

Separating the new Joule skills helps make each step in the process more reliable and easier to control. By keeping actions like searching tickets, finding solutions, and updating cases separate, we make sure Joule picks the right skill depending on the user’s request. This approach also ties closely to the importance of good skill descriptions: when each skill is focused and clearly described, Joule can match follow-up questions much more accurately. It keeps the conversation flow smooth and gives us the flexibility to tweak or improve individual skills later without disrupting the whole setup.

This use case shows how a custom Retrieval Augmented Generation (RAG) service can already be effectively integrated with Joule Studio, even without native support. As a result, support teams benefit from context-aware, AI-generated responses that boost efficiency and improve the overall customer service experience. By instantly retrieving and analyzing ticket data, Joule enables significantly faster issue resolution. It also supports proactive problem-solving by suggesting solutions based on similar past cases.

Future of AI Agents in Joule Studio

Looking ahead, building AI Agents within Joule Studio will be a key next step. As participants in the SAP Alpha Program for AI Agents, we already see strong potential to extend our use case even further. In the future, AI Agents could completely take over first-level support activities such as autonomously requesting additional information, suggesting possible solutions and reacting to further feedback by customers.

This will also require handling ticket escalations efficiently. In many service centers, one common challenge is identifying when a support ticket needs to be escalated. Service agents often have to manually decide if an issue is urgent enough to involve higher-level support, which can lead to delays or missed escalations. With large ticket volumes, it’s easy for critical issues to get lost among routine cases. AI Agents in Joule Studio could help by proactively monitoring tickets for escalation triggers and forwarding those tickets to the correct second- or third level support experts — taking service center efficiency to the next level. 

Our Final Thoughts on Joule Studio

Joule Studio is proving to be a promising addition to the SAP ecosystem. With the ability to extend capabilities using Custom Skills and integrate external services like sovanta GenAI DocumentChat, it offers a flexible and future-proof way to enhance business processes. Integrating Joule with AI Agents will open up exciting possibilities for realizing advanced use cases within Joule Studio, taking full advantage of multi-skill orchestration and autonomous task planning and handling. At sovanta, we’re excited to continue exploring how our clients can benefit from SAP Joule – today and in the future.

entry.title: SAP User Experience in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502
entry.content:
January 24 marked the general availability of the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502 release. As a cornerstone of modern enterprise, SAP Business Suite empowers organizations to scale, innovate, and adapt faster than ever. SAP cloud ERP applications, SAP Business AI, and SAP Business Data Cloud come together to deliver exceptional business value. But even the most robust cloud ERP wouldn't reach its full potential without a smooth user experience. That’s why we’re committed to evolving our product, listening to your feedback, and pushing the boundaries to deliver a user experience that matches the pace of today's dynamic work environment. Let me show you just how we achieve this in this release. Honorary mention: Generative AI is giving main character energy in this release.

Check out my video below to get a first glimpse into what’s coming in the area of SAP User Experience in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502:

 

Joule, the AI Copilot

Following a successful early adoption phase, Joule is generally available and fully integrated in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition. This AI copilot utilizes generative AI to help users complete tasks more efficiently by using natural language to express their business needs. It offers quick navigation to applications, instant insights into key business data like purchase orders, and concise user enablement content to speed up tasks. Joule also simplifies personalization of the My Home section, allowing users to create cards with critical data for immediate insights. Its capabilities include recommending relevant applications, providing insightful information on business situations without needing to open the application, and offering brief summaries of help content. Users can directly create and add preview cards to the Insights section of My Home.

 

 

AI-Assisted Smart Summarization

AI-assisted smart summarization allows you to summarize object pages of SAP Fiori elements-based applications with the power of generative AI. The summarization can be reviewed and modified in a rich text editor and easily copied into productivity tools, such as email or chat, for follow-ups with co-workers and customers. The tool also facilitates efficient sharing through seamless copy-to-clipboard functionality for quick follow-ups. Additionally, users have the option to provide feedback on their experience with the feature, contributing to its continual improvement and adaptation to user needs.

 

 

AI-Assisted Easy Filter

The AI-assisted easy filter leverages state-of-the-art generative AI to redefine how users approach filtering within SAP Fiori elements-based list reports. Rather than setting filter values manually through type-ahead inputs and value helps, you can simply type your search query into the filter field. This approach not only enhances the user experience but also provides easy access to recent and favorite filter queries. The AI results are reliable, offering an intuitive and click-efficient experience, which is particularly beneficial for new users navigating the system.

 

 

Currently in Beta Testing: AI-Assisted Easy Enterprise Search

The AI-assisted easy enterprise search leverages generative AI to enable natural language querying for business objects throughout the solution. By simply typing your search query into the search field of the SAP Fiori launchpad, users can quickly access the information they need. This feature is especially valuable for its time-saving capabilities when searching with known criteria, providing reliable AI results and offering an intuitive, click-efficient experience that benefits new users in particular.

Register here to start testing: Newest AI-assisted user experience features in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

 

Currently in Beta Testing: AI-Assisted Smart Personalization of My Home

The AI-assisted smart personalization of My Home allows you to use natural language through the power of generative AI to add insights cards to your My Home experience. This feature streamlines the process by enabling you to add insights cards directly from My Home with just two clicks, including a preview step for the end-user to review the AI-generated result. This tool offers several benefits, such as saving time when adding insights cards, providing better insights into business data, and delivering reliable AI results. It is designed to be intuitive and click-efficient, making it especially valuable for new users.

Register here to start testing: Newest AI-assisted user experience features in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

 

Currently in Beta Testing: AI-Assisted Error Explanation

AI-assisted error explanation supports in understanding errors in SAP Fiori elements-based applications better and provides first recommendations on how to solve the errors by leveraging the power of generative AI. This innovative feature is seamlessly integrated into the current error visualization, enabling users to generate explanations on demand, which significantly saves time and improves workflow efficiency. By offering intuitive navigation, it delivers both the explanatory insight and recommended solutions within a single, click-efficient action. The process includes well-structured results that clarify the nature of the error and suggest practical resolutions.

Register here to start testing: Newest AI-assisted user experience features in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

 

Interested in all things AI? Read this blog posts by my colleagues Yannick Peterschmitt and Sang Dao:

  • Artificial Intelligence and Other Intelligent ERP Use Cases in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502
  • Artificial Intelligence & Technologies in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502

 

To-Dos in Microsoft Teams Integration

The integration of To-Dos in Microsoft Teams allows users to seamlessly access their workflow items from SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition directly through the SAP S/4HANA Cloud for Microsoft Teams app. When you receive a workflow item in SAP S/4HANA Cloud, it triggers a notification in Microsoft Teams. This integration enables you to open the My Inbox application from Microsoft Teams to view details of the workflow items and to take immediate action. It also supports mobile approvals, ensuring flexibility in managing workflows on the go.

 

Smart Heads-Up of Situations in To Dos

Smart heads-up in To Dos ensure that you're always up to speed on situations requiring your attention, allowing you to manage all critical matters efficiently. This feature provides immediate insight and actionability through an intuitive interface, ensuring that nothing important slip through the cracks. By staying informed about and addressing potential issues early on, it significantly boosts productivity. Additionally, it offers support in prioritizing actions effectively. Users can get an overview of situations on both the standard and extended frameworks directly in the To-Dos section. With just one click, they can access detailed information by navigating to the My Situations and My Situations – Extended apps, depending on their needs.

 

As you can see, SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502 not only promises a transformed user experience but also enhances organizational productivity and strategic decision-making, bringing you closer to achieving seamless operational excellence, rooted in innovative technology.

Find out more about SAP User Experience in the numerous blog posts by my colleagues Thomas Reiss and Conrad Bernal.

The next release is just around the corner! See you in a bit. 😊

_____________________

The Future of Business, Today: Get Your Access to AI-Assisted Features Thanks to this Limited Promotion Now! 

The SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502 update is a strategic investment that combines advanced AI with powerful enterprise management tools. Whether scaling your business, enhancing productivity, or building sustainable operations, its capabilities help support a more agile and resilient future.

Sign up for following features through the limited Promotion for AI-assisted Features in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition (for all countries, customers, and partners):

  • The easy filter and smart summarization features in SAP Fiori allow for efficient, natural language filtering of business data and seamless summarization capabilities, enabling users to quickly extract relevant information and jump-start subsequent collaboration. 
  • Facilitate easier understanding and interpretation of financial data with insights from cost center reviews and explanations of fixed-asset depreciation keys.

Don’t hesitate to leave a comment and check out our community page to ask your questions and engage with the experts. Follow the PSCC_Enablement tag to stay up to date with our latest blog posts.

Feel free to follow me on SAP Community and on LinkedIn to not miss any updates.

📽️ Watch the Replays of Our SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502 Early Release Series!

From January 21 to January 24, we hosted a series of 21 compelling live sessions to highlight the exciting innovations shipped with the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502 release. Missed the live sessions? We've got you covered! Take advantage of the entire series or pick and choose the sessions most relevant to you. You will find a 43-minute-long recording and a presentation for SAP User Experience as well as 20 other topics of interest. Don't miss this chance and watch them on demand–anywhere, anytime.

entry.title: 7 Groundbreaking AI Announcements at SAP Sapphire 2025 You Shouldn’t Miss
entry.content:
At SAP Sapphire 2025, the spotlight was firmly on Artificial Intelligence — not as a future ambition, but as the new operational core of enterprise software. SAP unveiled a suite of innovations designed to make AI tangible, scalable, and deeply integrated into business processes.

Here’s a quick rundown of the 7 most exciting AI announcements you need to know.

1. Joule Is Now Omnipresent

SAP’s AI copilot, Joule, has evolved. It’s now context-aware, ever-present, and embedded across both SAP and non-SAP tools. With its new Action Bar, Joule can proactively recommend actions and automate tasks based on user behavior. It’s your AI assistant — wherever you work.

The biggest upgrade? A Perplexity AI partnership, allowing Joule to bring in real-time, verified insights from the public web to enhance its enterprise answers.

2. Agentic AI Goes Mainstream

SAP is rolling out an ecosystem of pre-built AI agents. These aren’t just tools — they’re task-oriented mini AIs built to manage workflows across HR, supply chain, finance, and sales. Examples include:

  • Shop Floor Supervisor Agent

  • Expense Validation Agent

  • Quote Creation & Catalog Optimization Agents

They can now even collaborate across systems, thanks to the new Agent2Agent protocol developed with Google.

3. AI Agent Hub + LeanIX Integration

Managing AI agents just got easier with the new AI Agent Hub and its connection to SAP LeanIX. This lets enterprises map each agent to a specific business process or capability — bringing structure and governance to your AI operations.

4. SAP AI Foundation Becomes the OS for AI

The newly launched SAP AI Foundation is like an operating system for enterprise AI. It includes:

  • AI Agent Runtime

  • Prompt Optimizer (make your prompts smarter & model-agnostic)

  • Joule Studio for agent and skill building

  • AI Evaluation Services

  • Enterprise-wide Knowledge Graphs

This foundation gives developers and IT teams the infrastructure to build, deploy, and scale AI applications confidently.

5. Business Data Cloud Gets Smarter

SAP’s Business Data Cloud isn’t just for analytics anymore. It’s now fueling intelligent applications like:

  • People Intelligence (HR insights from SuccessFactors)

  • Spend Control Tower

  • Green Ledger for sustainability tracking

With connections to Google BigQuery, AWS, and Azure, your data is ready for intelligent automation at scale.

6. Developer-Centric AI Enhancements

If you’re building on SAP, you’re in for a treat:

  • Low-code AI agent development via Joule Studio

  • AI-powered ABAP tooling for ECC migration

  • Custom LLM integration in the Integration Suite

  • Marketplace availability via AWS and GCP

SAP’s message is clear: AI should be easy to build and run, not a black box.

7. Real-Time Intelligence with Perplexity AI

One of the most intriguing partnerships is with Perplexity AI, giving SAP users access to real-time, web-validated information — integrated directly into their business queries.

This means you’re no longer limited to internal data. Joule can now combine enterprise knowledge with trusted external content for complete, contextual answers.

Final Thoughts

SAP Sapphire 2025 marked a pivotal shift — from talking about AI to operationalizing it at scale. Whether you’re in tech, operations, or strategy, these announcements reflect one reality: AI is now core to SAP — and core to the future of enterprise transformation.

entry.title: 🚀 秒速で学ぶ SAP AI Core Agent 開発 🚀 - Part 5 RAG 基礎 ②: Retriever Toolの構築
entry.content:
生成 AI は研究室の外へ飛び出し、今やビジネス現場の常識を塗り替えています。SAPは全速力でその波に乗っています。このブログシリーズでは、SAP AI Core の既定モデルを最速で呼び出し、実務で使える AI エージェントへ拡張する“秒速ハンズオン”をお届けします。

お知らせ
You can find the English version here.

 

📖 本シリーズで学べること

  • SAP AI Core 上でカスタム AI Agent を “秒速” で動かす方法
  • LangChain・Google 検索ツール・RAGを使った実装
  • AI Agent を REST API 化し、SAPUI5/Fiori の UI に載せ、Cloud Foundryにデプロイする手順

学習時間
各章は 10–15 分 で読める＆手を動かせるを予定しています。

 

🗺️ 連載ロードマップ

  • Part 0 プロローグ
  • Part 1 環境構築: SAP AI CoreとAI Launchpad
  • Part 2 LangChain でチャットモデルを構築
  • Part 3 Agentツール: Google 検索を統合
  • Part 4 RAG 基礎 ①: HANA Cloud VectorEngineと埋め込み処理
  • Part 5 RAG 基礎 ②: Retriever Toolの構築 [現在のブログ]
  • Part 6 AI Agent を REST API 化
  • Part 7 SAPUI5 でチャットUI を構築
  • Part 8 CloudFoundry にデプロイ

注記
続編は順次公開予定です。

この記事がお役に立ったら、ぜひ Kudos を押していただけると励みになります。 「ここをもっと詳しく知りたい」「別のテーマも取り上げてほしい」など、ご要望があればお気軽にコメントください！

RAG 基礎 ②: Retriever Toolの構築

1｜はじめに

前章では社内ドキュメントを SAP HANA Cloud VectorEngine に格納し、ベクトルによるクエリ（質問文）と社内ドキュメントとの類似度検索を実行するところまで完了しました。本章では、その検索ロジックを AI Agent が呼び出せるツール化（Retriever Tool 化）します。これにより、AI Agent は「社内ベクターストア → Web 検索」の二段ロケットで回答を組み立てられるようになります。

 

2 | 事前準備

  • BTP サブアカウント
  • SAP AI Core インスタンス
  • SAP AI Launchpadのサブスクリプション
  • Python 3.13環境 & pip
  • VSCodeやBASなどのIDE

Trial 環境の注意
Trial の HANA Cloud インスタンスは 毎晩自動停止 されます。日をまたぐ作業の場合は翌日インスタンスを再起動してください。

 

3 | Retriever Tool の作成

Retriever とは「クエリと内容が似ているテキストチャンクを返すオブジェクト」で、LangChain の RAG パイプラインで最初に呼ばれます。前章で作った dbインスタンスには既にベクトルとメタデータが保存されているため、これをそのまま Retriever へ昇格させます。

まず、前章で埋め込み処理&格納したデータを HANA Cloud Explorer 上で確認してみましょう。TEST_TABLE テーブルを開くと、テキスト列とベクトル列が格納されているのが確認できます。

 

LangChain における Retriever は .as_retriever() で簡単に生成できます。前章で作った db インスタンス（ベクターストア）をそのまま再利用しましょう。

# ▶ Notebook Cell 11
retriever = db.as_retriever()

# テストクエリを投げて 1 件だけ確認
retriever.invoke("Tell me about SAP security")[0]


続いて、Retriever を ツール化 します。create_retriever_tool に Retriever 本体・ツール名・説明文を渡すだけです。説明文は LLM が読んで使用可否を判断する重要パラメータなので、目的を明確に書きましょう。説明文には「何を検索できるか」「いつ使うべきか」を含めることをオススメします。

# ▶ Notebook Cell 12
from langchain.tools.retriever import create_retriever_tool
retriever_tool = create_retriever_tool(
    retriever=retriever,
    name="hana_vectorengine",
    description=(
        "Use this tool to search internal SAP documents stored in HANA Cloud Vector Engine when the user asks about company-specific policies, security, or best practices."
    ),
)

 

4 | AI Agent に組み込んで動かす

これで Google Search と HANA VectorEngine の 2 本立てツールセットが揃いました。
initialize_agent() で両方を渡して agent インスタンスを作成します。そして、複雑な質問を AI Agent に投げてみましょう！

# ▶ Notebook Cell 13
from langchain.agents import initialize_agent, AgentType

agent = initialize_agent(
    tools=[google_tool, retriever_tool],
    llm=chat_llm,
    agent=AgentType.OPENAI_FUNCTIONS,
    verbose=True,
)

result = agent.invoke("RISE with SAP のサービス内容と、2025 年時点でのクラウド移行市場シェア（他社比較）をあわせて解説してください。")

 

"verbose=True"と指定すると、AI Agent の『Thought → Action → Observation』の流れがそのまま出力されます。下のスクリーンショットにおける、

Invoking: hana_vectorengine with {'query': 'RISE with SAP サービス内容'}

で、まず HANA Cloud VectorEngine で社内ドキュメント検索を行っていることが分かります。
続く赤枠、

Invoking: google_search with {'query': '2025 クラウド移行市場シェア SAP 他社比較'}

で、Google Search による最新市場データの取得を表しています。
（環境によって実行順やクエリ文字列は多少変わる場合があります。）

 

最後に、エージェントの最終回答を Markdown として表示しましょう。
これで本章のゴール――「検索ツールと HANA Cloud VectorEngine による Retriever を併用する AI Agent」の完成です！！

# ▶ Notebook Cell 14
from IPython.display import Markdown, display

display(Markdown(result["output"]))

 

5｜チャレンジ – AI Agent に メモリ を付けてみよう

今回までに作った AI Agent は会話履歴を保持しません。これでは「さっきの質問を踏まえて答える」「ユーザ名を覚える」といった自然な対話ができないため、LangChain の従来メモリクラス ConversationBufferMemory を使って履歴を保存できるように拡張します。

将来的な移行について
ConversationBufferMemory は LangChain 0.3 系で非推奨となり、今後は LangGraph を用いたメモリ実装が推奨されています。
本シリーズではまず “秒速” で動くことを優先し、今回は従来クラスで実装します。後日 Part 5.5 で LangGraph 版の AI Agent ＋ メモリ実装 をあらためて作成し、移行手順まで詳しく取り上げる予定です。

# ▶ Notebook Cell 15
from langchain.chains.conversation.memory import ConversationBufferMemory
from langchain_core.prompts import MessagesPlaceholder
from langchain.agents import initialize_agent, AgentType

agent_kwargs = {
    "extra_prompt_messages": [MessagesPlaceholder(variable_name="memory")],
}

memory = ConversationBufferMemory(memory_key="memory", return_messages=True)

agent_with_memory = initialize_agent(
    tools=[google_tool, retriever_tool],
    llm=chat_llm,
    agent=AgentType.OPENAI_FUNCTIONS,
    verbose=True,
    agent_kwargs=agent_kwargs,
    memory=memory,
)

上のセルでは ①履歴を保持する ConversationBufferMemory を生成し、②initialize_agent に memory 引数として渡しています。MessagesPlaceholder を追加したことで、履歴がプロンプトに自動で差し込まれる仕組みになりました。

続いて動きを確認します。

result1 = agent_with_memory.invoke("私の名前は伊藤です。")
result2 = agent_with_memory.invoke("私の名前は？")
print(result2["output"])

2 回目の呼び出しで「あなたの名前は伊藤さんですね」など前の発話を反映した答えが返ってくれば、メモリが機能しています。出力から履歴が連鎖している様子を確認してみてください。 

メモリ付きになったことで、同じセッション内でのフォローアップ質問や、HANA Cloudでの検索結果・Google 検索結果を踏まえた追加質問が自然に行えるようになります。ぜひ色々試してみてください！

 

6｜次回予告

Part 6 AI Agent を REST API 化

Part 6 では、これまでに作成したNotebookのセルを繋げて、１つのWeb APIとしてまとめます。また、作成したAI Agent APIのテストも実施してみます。お楽しみに！

 

免責事項

本ブログに記載された見解および意見はすべて私個人のものであり、私の個人的な立場で発信しています。SAP は本ブログの内容について一切の責任を負いません。

entry.content:
The Release Navigator for SAP BTP continues to evolve and expand, offering our customers improved resources and streamlined access to product updates. As we step into Q2 2025, we are excited to announce the addition of 11 new product pages. Read more about the details in this blog post.

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

Collaborating closely with SAP BTP experts, we've expanded the release coverage by introducing 11 new product pages. These pages compile links to all available release information, such as schedules, pre- and post-release communications, blogs, and more:

entry.title: AI in Action: New mission available to enhance SAP Signavio with Machine Learning
entry.content:
In today's rapidly evolving business landscape, organizations are continually seeking innovative ways to enhance operational efficiency and stay ahead of the competition. One groundbreaking approach is the integration of machine learning (ML) with process mining, a strategy that transforms static data into dynamic, predictive insights. SAP Signavio Process Intelligence, in conjunction with SAP Build, offers a robust platform to achieve this integration seamlessly.

Unlocking Predictive Process Management

Traditionally, process mining involves analyzing historical event logs to understand past performance. While this retrospective analysis is valuable, it doesn't provide foresight into future outcomes. By embedding ML algorithms into your process mining activities, you can predict future events, identify potential bottlenecks before they occur, and make data-driven decisions that proactively enhance process efficiency.

Practical Applications

Consider the challenge of predicting sales order delivery times. By analyzing historical event logs, ML models can forecast delivery dates based on factors like order size, product type, and current workload. These predictions, when fed back into SAP Signavio Process Intelligence, offer real-time insights, enabling better planning and improved customer satisfaction.

Another application is in customer support. By examining event logs, ML can predict customer satisfaction levels with support tickets, considering variables such as resolution speed and communication quality. This allows support teams to proactively address issues, enhancing the overall customer experience.

New mission OUT NOW

We are excited to announce a new mission that showcases how you can realize these use cases using SAP Signavio in combination with SAP Build.

https://developers.sap.com/mission.signavio-pi-and-sap-build.html 

Successfully implement the mission and earn a cool badge as a reward.

 

It will highlight your success of combining multiple SAP solutions and showcase your ability to tackle new topics and realize value from it!

Seamless Integration with SAP Build

SAP Build, equipped with Python and Jupyter capabilities, serves as the bridge between raw data and actionable insights. It enables the extraction of event log data from SAP Signavio Process Intelligence, the application of ML algorithms, and the reintegration of enriched data back into the system. This seamless workflow ensures that your process management system evolves from reactive to proactive, continually learning and adapting to new data.

Getting Started

Embarking on this transformative journey involves a few key steps:

  1. Activate APIs: Enable the OData and Ingestion APIs in SAP Signavio Process Intelligence to facilitate data movement.
  2. Data Extraction: Utilize SAP Build to extract event log data via the OData API.
  3. Apply Machine Learning: Develop and train ML models within SAP Build to analyze and predict outcomes based on your data.
  4. Data Reintegration: Push the enriched, predictive data back into SAP Signavio Process Intelligence through the Ingestion API.

    For a comprehensive, step-by-step guide on implementing this integration, refer to the mission above.

    By harnessing the combined power of SAP Signavio Process Intelligence and SAP Build, your organization can transition to a predictive process management paradigm, driving efficiency, foresight, and a competitive edge in your industry.

entry.title: 🚀 SAP AI Core Agent QuickLaunch Series 🚀 - Part 5 RAG Basics ②: Building Retriever Tool
entry.content:
Generative AI has leapt from research papers to daily business reality— and SAP is surfing that wave at full speed. In this hands‑on series, I’ll show you how to spin up a custom AI agent on SAP AI Core in minutes, then grow it into a production‑ready asset—without drowning in theory.

Notice
日本語版はこちらです。

 

📖 What You’ll Learn in This Series

  • How to Run a Custom AI Agent on SAP AI Core in Seconds
  • Implementation Using LangChain, Google Search Tool, and RAG
  • Steps to Convert the AI Agent into a REST API, Integrate It into an SAPUI5/Fiori UI, and Deploy to Cloud Foundry

Time Commitment
Each part is designed to be completed in  10–15 minutes .

 

🗺 ️ Series Roadmap

  • Part 0 Prologue
  • Part 1 Env Setup: SAP AICore & AI Launchpad
  • Part 2 Building a Chat Model with LangChain
  • Part 3 Agent Tools: Integrating Google Search
  • Part4 RAG Basics① HANA Cloud VectorEngine & Embedding
  • Part 5 RAG Basics ②: Building Retriever Tool [current blog]
  • Part 6: Converting the AI Agent into a REST API
  • Part 7: Building the Chat UI with SAPUI5
  • Part 8: Deploying to Cloud Foundry

Note
Subsequent blogs will be published soon.

If you enjoyed this post, please give it a  kudos! Your support really motivates me. Also, if there’s anything you’d like to know more about, feel free to leave a comment!

RAG Basics ②: Building Retriever Tool

1 | Introduction

In the previous chapter, we stored our internal documents in the SAP HANA Cloud Vector Engine and completed the similarity search between user queries (as vectors) and those documents. In this chapter, we’ll turn that search logic into a tool that our AI agent can invoke (a Retriever Tool). With this in place, the AI agent will be able to build its responses using a two-stage “internal vector store → web search” process.

 

2 | Prerequisites

  • BTP sub-account
  • SAP AI Core instance
  • SAP AI LaunchPad Subscription
  • Python 3.13 and pip
  • VSCode, BAS or any IDE

Note for the Trial Environment
The HANA Cloud instance in the Trial enviroment automatically shuts down every night. If your work spans past midnight, please restart the instance the following day.

 

3 | Build the Retriever Tool

A retriever is an object that returns text chunks whose content closely matches a given query, and it’s the first component invoked in LangChain’s RAG pipeline. Because our DB instance from the previous chapter already contains both vectors and metadata, we can promote it directly to a retriever.

First, let’s inspect the data we embedded and stored in the last chapter via the HANA Cloud Explorer. If you open the TEST_TABLE table, you’ll see that it holds both the text column and its corresponding vector column.

 

In LangChain, you can turn your vector store into a Retriever in one line using the .as_retriever() method. Let’s reuse the db instance we created in the previous chapter:

# ▶ Notebook Cell 11
retriever = db.as_retriever()

# Send a test query and retrieve just one result
retriever.invoke("Tell me about SAP security")[0]

 

Next, we’ll package this Retriever as a tool. LangChain’s create_retriever_tool helper takes three arguments—the Retriever itself, a tool name, and a description. Because the description is what the LLM reads to decide when to invoke the tool, be sure to state clearly what this tool searches and when it should be used. Here’s an example:

# ▶ Notebook Cell 12
from langchain.tools.retriever import create_retriever_tool
retriever_tool = create_retriever_tool(
    retriever=retriever,
    name="hana_vectorengine",
    description=(
        "Use this tool to search internal SAP documents stored in HANA Cloud Vector Engine when the user asks about company-specific policies, security, or best practices."
    ),
)

 

4 | Integrate and Running in the AI Agent

Now that we have a two-tool setup—Google Search and our HANA VectorEngine retriever—we can wire them into an AI Agent. Pass both tools into initialize_agent() to create the agent instance, then fire off a complex query:

# ▶ Notebook Cell 13
from langchain.agents import initialize_agent, AgentType

agent = initialize_agent(
    tools=[google_tool, retriever_tool],
    llm=chat_llm,
    agent=AgentType.OPENAI_FUNCTIONS,
    verbose=True,
)

result = agent.invoke(
    "Please explain the services offered by RISE with SAP and, as of 2025, the cloud-migration market share compared to other vendors."
)

 

By setting verbose=True, you’ll see the agent’s full “Thought → Action → Observation” trace. For example, in the screenshot below you’ll spot:

Invoking: `google_search` with `cloud migration market share 2025 SAP compared to other vendors`

which shows the agent pulling the latest market‐share data via Google Search, followed by:

Invoking: `hana_vectorengine` with `{'query': 'services offered by RISE with SAP'}`

indicating that it’s then querying your internal SAP documents in HANA Cloud VectorEngine. (The exact order or query phrasing may vary slightly by environment.)

  

Finally, let’s render the agent’s final answer as Markdown. With this step, we’ve completed our chapter goal—an AI Agent that combines a web search tool with the HANA Cloud VectorEngine Retriever!

# ▶ Notebook Cell 14
from IPython.display import Markdown, display

display(Markdown(result["output"]))

 

5 | Challenge – Add Memory to the AI Agent

So far, the AI Agent we’ve built does not retain any conversation history. Without this, it can’t handle natural dialogue features like “answer based on the previous question” or “remember the user’s name.” To fix that, we’ll extend it to store its history using LangChain’s conventional ConversationBufferMemory class.

Future migration note:
ConversationBufferMemory is deprecated in LangChain 0.3, and going forward, memory implementations based on LangGraph are recommended. In this series, we’re prioritizing “speed to implementation,” so we’ll stick with the traditional class for now. In Part 5.5, we’ll revisit this and build an AI Agent with LangGraph–based memory, covering the migration steps in detail.

# ▶ Notebook Cell 15
from langchain.chains.conversation.memory import ConversationBufferMemory
from langchain_core.prompts import MessagesPlaceholder
from langchain.agents import initialize_agent, AgentType

agent_kwargs = {
    "extra_prompt_messages": [MessagesPlaceholder(variable_name="memory")],
}

memory = ConversationBufferMemory(memory_key="memory", return_messages=True)

agent_with_memory = initialize_agent(
    tools=[google_tool, retriever_tool],
    llm=chat_llm,
    agent=AgentType.OPENAI_FUNCTIONS,
    verbose=True,
    agent_kwargs=agent_kwargs,
    memory=memory,
)

In the cell above, we did two things:

  1. Created a ConversationBufferMemory instance to hold the conversation history.

  2. Passed that memory into initialize_agent.

By including a MessagesPlaceholder, LangChain will now automatically inject the stored history into each prompt. 

Next, let’s verify that it’s working:

result1 = agent_with_memory.invoke("My name is Ryota")
result2 = agent_with_memory.invoke("Who am I")
print(result2["output"])

On the second call, you should see a reply like “Your name is Mr. Ito,” which shows that the agent remembered your earlier message. Check the output to confirm that the history is being threaded through.

With memory enabled, you can now naturally ask follow-up questions in the same session, or build on results from HANA Cloud searches and Google searches. Give it a try!

 

7 | Next Up

Part 6: Converting the AI Agent into a REST API

In Part 6, we’ll link together the notebooks we’ve created so far to turn them into a web API, and we’ll also run tests on the AI Agent API we’ve developed. Stay tuned!

 

Disclaimer

All the views and opinions in the blog are my own and is made in my personal capacity and that SAP shall not be responsible or liable for any of the contents published in this blog.

entry.title: MaCo Onboarding Agent: Exploring its Multi-Agent AI Architecture
entry.content:
By Sonam Singh, Francisco Freitas

Overview

As AI-driven transformation reshapes enterprise systems, intelligent onboarding becomes essential for improving customer experience and accelerating product adoption. In MaCo (SAP Market Communication for Utilities), we are driving an innovation initiative by developing the MaCo Onboarding Agent—a multi-agent AI solution.

The MaCo Onboarding Agent is designed to engage with customers before they subscribe to MaCo saas. On request, the agent is intended to collect essential information such as Global Account, subaccount, and subdomain, and generate an onboarding incident to initiate and manage the onboarding process. This removes manual overhead, especially for customers with multiple subscriptions.

Using a vectorized knowledge base from MaCo documentation, downstream service documentation, and FAQs, the assistant offers real-time, context-specific support. For example, based on the customer's monthly message volume, the assistant can recommend the appropriate subscription plan, for testing or production use, by referencing the relevant information maintained in the FAQs. This ensures customers receive timely and contextually accurate guidance, improving their overall experience.

Architecture: Engineered for Scalability, Modularity, and Intelligence

The MaCo Onboarding Agent is built on a supervisor-level multi-agent architecture, designed with a modular and extensible technology stack:

  • Python with LangChain and LangGraph: Serves as the orchestration layer for agent-based interactions. LangChain provides structured tooling for chaining LLM prompts and external calls, while LangGraph introduces persistent, event-driven workflows with stateful memory management and multi-agent routing logic, exposed as a RESTful API.
  • SAP CAP (Cloud Application Programming) using Node.js
  • SAP BTP (Business Technology Platform)
  • SAP HANA for Semantic Search and Context Retrieval 
    MaCo documentation, downstream service documentations and references, and FAQs are converted into vector representations stored in SAP HANA's vector database. This allows the assistant to perform semantic searches and retrieve information based on meaning rather than exact keywords. At runtime, relevant content is dynamically retrieved from the vector store to build context for responses.
  • Multi-Model Support (e.g., GPT-4.0, Gemini): Enables runtime selection of multiple large language models based on use case requirements such as accuracy vs. latency trade-offs, tokens constraints, and response time requirements. The classification logic is also handled by LLMs, based on the prompt and input context.

Supervisor-Agent Routing Flow

Supervisor Agent: This agent acts as the orchestrator. It receives user inputs—ranging from questions to actionable requests, to determine an appropriate response strategy. To do this effectively, it uses structured prompts, including simulated human messages, to guide the language model's classification. Depending on the nature of the query, it may engage one or more of the specialized sub-agents.

Knowledge Retrieval Agent: This RAG agent utilizes vectorized knowledge base to search for relevant information chunks, followed by summarization using an LLM to provide a context-aware response.

Incident Management Agent: This agent handles actionable user requests related to incidents, such as creating, updating, or retrieving incidents based on processing logic and integrated system rules.

Fallback Agent: This agent is engaged when the user query is unclear, out of scope, or lacks relevant information. It helps maintain a meaningful conversation by steering the dialogue in a helpful direction.

User Interface

 

 

What’s in Progress

The MaCo Onboarding Agent extends its capabilities beyond a standard chatbot assistant. Integration with JIRA and ServiceNow APIs is also underway, enabling a dedicated agent to create and manage incidents.

Business Benefits

Efficient Onboarding Process

  • Accelerates the onboarding process by providing context-specific support and resolving queries in real-time
  • Particularly valuable for enterprise customers with high-volume onboarding requirements by automating manual tasks.

24/7 Real-Time Support

  • Leverages a vectorized knowledge base for real-time, context-aware assistance.
  • Once subscribed, customers go through multiple setup steps. The assistant provides on-demand access to knowledge from MaCo documentation, downstream service references, and FAQs — enabling users to resolve known issues or questions independently. This reduces reliance on support teams and minimizes delays caused by time zone differences or limited team availability.

Continuous Customer Engagement

  • Keeps customers informed on ongoing issues with downstream services or MaCo issues by gathering information from FAQs.
  • Ensures ongoing transparency and trust during the onboarding lifecycle.

Conclusion

The MaCo Onboarding Agent demonstrates how intelligent automation and a multi-agent solution can enhance the efficiency and consistency of early customer interactions. By providing access to relevant information and supporting decision-making with contextual guidance, the solution enables a more scalable and responsive customer experience.

Ongoing enhancements would explore extended capabilities, including deeper integrations, improved coordination between agents, and alignment with evolving customer and product needs.

entry.title: SAP CodeJam Roadshow 2025 - Latin America edition 🇧🇷 🇵🇪 🇲🇽 🇨🇴 (July 25th - Aug 12th 2025)
entry.content:
¡Hola SAP Developers!

I've got some exciting news to share with you. The SAP CodeJam roadshow is coming back to Latin America! This time, Josh Bentley, Rich Heilman, Thomas Jung, and Antonio Maradiaga will be part of the second SAP CodeJam Roadshow in Latin America.  We will have four stops on the roadshow starting the 25th of July and then continuing from August 4th - 12th, 2025, doing a total of seven CodeJams, one in São Paulo and then two in Lima, Mexico City and Bogota. Also, we'll organise a couple of quedadas (meetups) as we did last year. :-).

Check out the featured guests list in each event to learn which Developer Advocate(s) will be at the different events/locations.

 

SAP CodeJam ABAP Cloud

This SAP CodeJam event is on the topic of the ABAP Cloud, and specifically introduces attendees to ABAP Cloud and the ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model. ABAP Cloud is the ABAP based development model for S/4HANA Cloud, S/4HANA on premise, and SAP BTP, ABAP environment. Learn how to use proven and new technologies, such as Core Data Services (CDS) and behavior definition and implementation languages, to build SAP Fiori apps and OData services on any of the ABAP Cloud options. In this hands-on experience, developers will create their first ABAP applications using ABAP Cloud, expose and consume services via HTTP, and explore the ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model.

Getting started with Generative AI Hub on SAP AI Core

In this CodeJam, you will learn how to use Generative AI Hub on SAP AI Core to implement a retrieval augmented generation (RAG) use case to improve the responses of large language models (LLMs) and reduce hallucinations. You will learn how to deploy an LLM and the orchestration service on SAP AI Core and query it via SAP AI Launchpad and the Python SDK. Furthermore, you will learn about the most important genAI concepts and create and use text chunks and embeddings to improve your RAG response.

SAP CodeJam events and quedadas (meetups) part of the roadshow (Register Now!)

Below is a listing of the SAP CodeJam events and quedadas (meetups) on the roadshow schedule:

entry.title: My AI Experience Sharing – Setting up Joule for the S/4HANA Public Edition (Part A: Preparation)
entry.content:
Introduction

Now Artificial Intelligence (AI) is on every professional’s mind. Everybody wants to be on the bandwagon.  I plan to write a series of blogs on this topic under a big umbrella “My AI Experience Sharing”.  This is the first one: setting up Joule for the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition. 

I purposely delayed my action on setting up a Joule to wait for the dust settling down during the Early Adopter phase. The intention paid off but still won’t give me a free pass . There are five credible references available as listed in the Reference section, but none of them give you a good explanation on Why, especially on What in a critical step with a readable picture when you desperately need a hint.  To help all the people like me, this blog will focus on explaining those steps, what to enter, and possible mistakes to avoid. 

Note 1: the content here is drawn from my direct experience from setting up Joule for the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition. It might be true for other SAP solutions but only use that as a reference so that I don’t feel the pressure of entire SAP world on my shoulder 😀.

Note 2: Since there are a lot of topics to discuss, I divided the blog into Part A: Preparation and Part B: Joule Installation.  Keep in mind that Joule Setup includes both Preparation and Joule Installation. Without Preparation work, Joule won’t work!

Note 3: The systems I used in this blog are:

  • BTP Global Account: 2024-09 3SL Account
  • BTP Joule Subaccount: PSCC-AMER-3SL-PRD
  • Public Cloud Tenant: Production tenant G4X/100, http://myxxxxxxx.s4hana.cloud.sap/ 
  • Cloud Identity Services: Provisioned together with above BTP and Public Cloud tenants, xxxxxxxx.accounts.ondemand.com

 

Solution Architecture for Joule Running with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

Joule is an SAP AI Co-Pilot developed by SAP to work seamlessly with SAP solutions, such as the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition (I will call it the Public Cloud for easy writing and reading).  It can guide you through content discovery in a format of natural language dialog (aka Generative AI).

To make the Joule working, we need to integrate SAP solution, Application Clients, SAP Cloud Identity Service (CIS) and SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) as shown below. In addition, Joule integrates with the SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition.  Essentially, Joule acts as a conversational interface within the Work Zone, empowering users to interact with SAP systems more intuitively and efficiently.

Solution Architecture for Joule Running with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

At a very high level, we can highlight some key points of the Solution Architecture to help us understand those steps to take in the set-up procedures:

  1. User access either the Public Cloud or the Joule through the Application Client, such as the web client.
  2. There is a Joule UI plug-in within the Application Client to access Joule.
  3. When a dialog is initiated in Joule, the later access the Public Cloud data through an OData API (green line).
  4. To make this API call successful, there is a pre-built trust between the Identity Authentication Service (IAS) and the Public Cloud, as well as the BTP. This needs to be configured.  Trust enables the SSO.
  5. Joule is integrated into the SAP Work Zone, enabling users to access Joule’s capabilities within their Work Zone environment.  Work Zone has access to the Public Cloud data.
  6. Provide the Content Data Model (CDM) meta data to the Work Zone

One important thing here is that all three components (Public Cloud tenants, Identity Authentication Service Tenant and the SAP BTP Global Account) are associated with the same Customer Number to simplify the configuration. If this is not the case for you, see Reuse SAP Cloud Identity Services Tenants for Different Customer IDs in the documentation for Identity Authentication.  This is discussed in SAP Help Configure Trust to the Identity Authentication Tenant.

 

Major Setup Steps

By combining the information provided from five different references, I list 17 major steps, eight of them for preparation and nine for Joule Installation as shown in below two tables. Each table has four columns: Step (number), App (to run the step), Action (of the step) and Purpose (of the step).  You can use these two tables to guide you through the entire setup process.

Here are the abbreviations listed in each step:

  • DC1: Discovery Center: Establish a Unified Joule Instance
  • DC2: Discovery Center: Activate Joule with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
  • Blog1: Joule – End-to-End Setup Guide (for all Line of Business) Unified Approach
  • Blog2: Joule in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition - Setup Guide
  • SH: SAP Help: Joule onboarding guide

I mostly follow the steps outlined in the DC1 for Preparation and DC2 for Joule Installation, with the complimentary content from other references. Two blogs are informative, but screenshots are impossible to read.  The SAP Help is not sufficient to guide a successful setup.

Eight Major Preparation Steps for Joule Setup in the Public Cloud

 

Ten Major Joule Installation Steps for Joule Setup in the Public Cloud

To make a sense of these steps, I pin the Steps A and Steps B on the original solution architecture diagram.

Major Preparation and Joule Installation Steps for Joule Setup in the Public Cloud

 

Eight Major Preparation Steps Explained

In this section, I am going to explain all of preparation steps listed.  The hyperlink of each step gives a direct access to the document.

We can group Preparation Steps into five categories:

  • Steps A.1 – A.2: Verify all necessary information
  • Step A.3: Create a BTP subaccount for Joule installation
  • Steps A.4 – A.6: Build trusts among BTP, IAS and Public Cloud
  • Step A.7: Build and subscribe Work Zone
  • Step A.8: Add missing user attributes to BTP applications

 

Step A.1 DC1: Verify Global Account Entitlements

When you sign the contract to SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, there are many line items called entitlements.  For example, SKU 8017178 is for Joule. With that, you can have Joule Entitlements in your BTP system. Make sure you have all the necessary entitlements before starting the set-up work, because it does take time to request additional entitlements if they are not there.

In my BTP Global Account “2024-09 3SL Account”, I have two Joule entitlement related plans (das stands for Digital Assistant Service; Joule is a product from SAP to serve the purpose of the Digital Assistant Service):

  1. das-application-canary
  2. das-application

The first plan was added in early April, 2025 after a change in Joule Booster by SAP. I used the 2nd Plan in March when there was only one Joule Booster.  In May, I had to use the 1st Plan to build another system.  I will discuss more about these plans later.

Joule Entitlements in the BTP Global Account

Besides Joule entitlement, you need SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition entitlement.  There is a difference between a foundation plan and a standard plan.  We will discuss later.

Entitlements of SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition

There are two Service Technical Names under the service SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition.  If you only have the build-workzone-standard plan, no SAPLaunchpad plan, no worry.  It worked for me as well.

The entitlements are added during the BTP system provision based on your CRM order (contract) information.  If you don’t have the necessary entitlements, contact your sales executive to correct it. 

 

Step A.2 DC1: Check S/4 HANA Public Cloud Tenant is Registered in BTP

This step is to build a communication binding between the BTP and the Public Cloud tenant with the following steps. The work is done on both sides: the BTP side as well as the Public Cloud Tenant side.

On the BTP side

Check if the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition tenant is registered under the System Landscape  System.  If your Public Cloud tenant is provisioned together with the BTP tenant, most probably, it is Auto-discovered and Registered.  Otherwise, just manually add the tenant here.

My four tenants are all registered as shown.

The SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Tenant is Registered in the BTP

 

On the Public Cloud Tenant Side

When the Public Cloud tenant is registered in the BTP, an Integration Token is generated. This Integration Token is used to create the Extension on the Public Cloud tenant side.  This way a solid binding is created between a Registered Public Cloud tenant in the BTP System Landscape and an Extension in the Public Cloud tenant.

An Extension for the Public Cloud Tenant Registered in the BTP

If you deregister the Public Cloud tenant in the BTP, the extension is removed on the Public Cloud tenant side automatically.  Then you need to re-register the system and get an integration token on the BTP; Re-create a new extension on the Public Cloud tenant.

 

Step A.3 Configure Subaccount in SAP BTP for Joule

In this step, we have three distinctive tasks:

  1. Create a dedicated Joule subaccount in the BTP Global Account.
    1. Create a subaccount (for this blog, my global account name is 2024-09 3SL Account, the subaccount name is PSCC-3SL-AMER-PRD)
    2. Enable the Cloud Foundry
    3. Create a Cloud Foundry space
  2. Create a Service Plan with the SAP Build Work Zone entitlements
  3. Add the Public Cloud tenant’s URL to the Trusted Domains list in the BTP subaccount.

The Cloud Foundry is a cloud application platform for building, testing, deploying, and scaling applications. In our case, we use it for Joule and Build Work Zone. By default, we get 200 GB space for it.

To add Service Plans to the SAP Build Work Zone, make sure you select below three plans for the Public Cloud: standard and standard (Application) instead of foundation and foundation (Application).  For the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, we don’t use any foundation plan for the Work Zone (see Blog 1: Joule – End-to-End Setup Guide (for all Line of Business) Unified Approach)

Here is the screenshot when you Add Service Plans.

Select Three Plans for the SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition

For the Trusted Domains, we add the URL like shown in the figure below.

Add the URL of the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition to the Trusted Domains List

Notes:

  1. When a subaccount is created in BTP, a new application is created in the IAS. However, it takes some time before the new application shows up. After it shows up, follow Step A.8 to add a new attribute Email_verified.
  2. When deleting a subaccount, make sure deleting all subscriptions, instances and environment first within the subaccount, then remove the subaccount itself.

 

As described at the Solution Architecture above, we need to establish a Trust among all three components: SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, the Identity Authentication Service Tenant and the SAP BTP. In other words, we want to be able to use the Single Signed-On (SSO) to access one component from another without explicitly providing a username and the password.  The following two steps try to accomplish this.

  1. Step A.4: Add identity providers for applications to enable users to log on to applications consumed in this subaccount.
  2. Step A.5: Add identity providers for platform users used by administrators, operators, and developers to access platform tools and environments.

 

Step A.4 DC1: Establish Trust between Subaccount and SAP Cloud Identity Authentication Service

This step is to establish a trust between the BTP subaccount and the SAP Cloud Identity Authentication Service (IAS) to ensure a Single Sign-on (SSO) works.  Let me explain how this SSO works as illustrated in three steps in the below picture.

1: When a user clicks on the Joule icon, there is a logon process happening.  Since Joule is a plug in running on the SAP Build Work Zone, this task is in fact a Work Zone task.

2: Work Zone is part of the BTP subaccount, so it reaches out to the Trust information stored to find out which Identity Authentication Service it can rely on.  In this Joule Setup, it is the IAS tenant we are trying to store in this Step A.4.

3: The IAS verifies the user. If the user exists, the logon happens behind the scenes without your input. This is called Single Sign-On.  If the user does not exist, the logon fails, or the SSO fails, and a logon window pops up asking you to enter the user information. If a wrong IAS is stored as a Trust IAS, the logon fails as well (this is what we will discuss in length about the domain name below).

The Single Sign-On Explained for Joule Logon

This step is also presented in SAP Help: Joule onboarding guide - Configure Trust to the Identity Authentication Tenant and Blog: Joule – End-to-End Setup Guide (for all Line of Business) Unified Approach.  In SAP Help Configure Trust to the Identity Authentication Tenant, it has a note: “For single-sign on to work, the domain of the Identity Authentication tenant must be same as the corresponding SAP system trust setup.” 

In Section 7. Setup Trust Configuration with SAP Cloud Identity Service of the Blog, it has a good discussion about how to choose the domain name, and I am copying it here to highlight its importance.

(P)lease validate if your SAP applications URL is using the domain model “*.accounts.cloud.sap” or do you see “*.accounts.ondemand.com”, select the appropriate domain, and click Next.

Example:

SAP S/4HANA Cloud: http://myxxxxxxx.s4hana.cloud.sap/

In my case, all my SAP applications are using *.accounts.cloud.sap so I have opted for *.cloud.sap.

Both references talk about the same thing, but hard to understand!!!  Here is why: our IAS tenant for the Public Cloud is xxxx.accounts400.ondemand.com, not xxxx.accounts400.cloud.sap.  For a reason I still cannot find (!), you cannot use the Public Cloud IAS URL as the domain name as it is.  It is a twist to test my brain power 😀.

Enough explanation.  Let’s look at the Configure Tenant dialog box. In the dropdown list of the Domain, you have two choices/formats, the xxxx.accounts400.ondemand.com and the xxxx.accounts400.cloud.sap.  Which one to use? For the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, our tenant is in the format of myxxxxxx.s4hana.cloud.sap, so to be consistent, the domain name xxxx.accounts400.cloud.sap should be selected. For some other applications like SAP Success Factors, the xxxx.accounts400.ondemand.com is proper when the cloud application URL uses the format of xxxx.ondemand.com.

Choose a Proper Domain Format in the Configure Tenant Dialog Box

The newly created Trust is part of Custom Identity Provider for Applications in the subaccount as shown in the red box below.  All the Properties of this Trust are displayed on the right-hand side.  The red box highlights the Domain name xxxx.accounts400.cloud.sap.  You can Edit or Delete this Trust entry.

The Established Trust between the BTP Subaccount and the IAS

After the Trust is established, an application called SAP BTP subaccount PSCC-AMER-3SL-PRD is created in the SAP Cloud IAS.  Now we can use the SAP CIS as an identity provider to authenticate the users from this BTP subaccount. All the SSO related settings, such as Subject Name Identifier and Attributes, are present there. Details to be discussed in Step A.8.

The BTP Subaccount PSCC-AMER-3SL-PRD related application in the SAP Cloud IAS

Let me show you an example how this Trust doesn’t work: When you click on the Joule icon in the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, based on your user information, the system tries to logon to the Joule service on the BTP subaccount with a Single Sign-on.  The authentication is carried out by your IAS on behalf of the BTP subaccount.  When a wrong IAS domain is provided (I purposely used the domain name “*.accounts.ondemand.com”), the SSO cannot carry on, and the below Sign In window pops up.  From the text string below Sign In, you can clearly identify that it is for das-ias (a Joule application, or a Digital Assistant Service), on the BTP subaccount 2199xxxxxxxx.

A Joule Sign-In Window Pops Up when the SSO Fails

After you enter your username and the password, Joule will launch for you. But it is tedious as each time you need to enter the user credentials when accessing Joule.  That’s the reason we want to use SSO to bypass the sign in process and have a better end user experience.

 

Step A.5 Blog1: 7.1 Trust the Public Cloud Tenant Domain in Your BTP Subaccount

In Blog 1 Joule – End-to-End Setup Guide (for all Line of Business) Unified Approach and SAP Help Joule onboarding guide, they all discussed building trust domains in Identity Authentication Service Tenant and the BTP subaccount. But I don’t see this step in the Discovery Zone.

For this step, I added my SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition tenant’s URL here as I don’t have other applications for this configuration.  If you have multiple applications, you can either list them separately or use a wild card like *.cloud.sap. 

Note: Leave the “https://” here to be a full URL (Uniform Resource Locator) or address of your application tenant.
